responsibilities: Review invoice details (supplier, amount, date, line items)
Flag exceptions and missing delivery records
Submit approved invoices for payment scheduling
Approve payments between R5,000 and R50,000
Review and sign off reconciliation reports

orgMap.confidence_reason: Roles, approval thresholds, and document types were explicitly described. Director role inferred from payment hierarchy.

workflows.trigger: New supplier invoice received via email by Admin
steps: Admin receives invoice email and forwards to Finance Assistant
Finance Assistant extracts invoice details (supplier, amount, date, line items)
Finance Assistant searches delivery records log for matching delivery
If no delivery record found: flag invoice, notify supplier, hold payment
If delivery record found: verify amount matches purchase order
If amount mismatch: hold and escalate to Operations Manager
If amount matches and invoice < R5,000: Finance Assistant approves
If invoice R5,000–R50,000: submit to Finance Manager for approval
If invoice > R50,000 or international: submit to Director for approval
Approved invoice added to payment schedule
workflows.confidence_reason: Workflow steps, roles, and thresholds were explicitly described in detail
missing_information: Who notifies the supplier of missing delivery record?
What is the SLA for supplier response on disputes?

steps: Finance Assistant downloads bank statement
Finance Assistant opens general ledger in Excel
Finance Assistant manually matches bank transactions to ledger entries
Unmatched items listed as exceptions
Exceptions reviewed by Finance Manager
Discrepancies above R10,000 escalated to Director
Finance Manager signs off reconciliation report
Report filed
workflows.confidence_reason: Process steps described but timing and SLA not specified
missing_information: How long does reconciliation typically take?
Is there a formal sign-off document or just verbal approval?

workflows.trigger: Invoice exception detected during approval workflow
steps: Exception type identified (missing record, duplicate, amount mismatch, unknown supplier)
Finance Assistant logs exception
For missing delivery record: notify supplier and hold payment
For duplicate invoice: reject and notify supplier
For amount mismatch: escalate to Operations Manager with PO reference
For unrecognised supplier: escalate to Finance Manager for verification
Resolution documented
Invoice either approved, rejected, or held pending resolution
workflows.confidence_reason: Exception types listed but resolution steps are partially implied, not fully defined
missing_information: What is the escalation SLA for each exception type?
Who has authority to reject a supplier permanently?
Is there a dispute log or tracking system?

rules.source: Payments under R5,000: Finance Assistant can approve
Payments R5,000 to R50,000: Finance Manager approval required
Payments above R50,000: Director sign-off required
All international payments: Director sign-off required regardless of amount
Any supplier invoice without a matching delivery record must be flagged
Any discrepancies above R10,000 are escalated to Director
Duplicate invoice: Reject and notify supplier

risks.risk: Invoices may be paid without delivery verification due to manual matching
risks.reason: The delivery matching step is entirely manual. Human error could cause payments to be made for goods never received.
risks.recommendation: Implement automated invoice-to-delivery-record matching before human review

risks.risk: Single point of failure in Finance Assistant role
risks.reason: The Finance Assistant is the only person performing invoice checks, matching, and reconciliation. No documented backup or cover process.
risks.recommendation: Document a cover process and cross-train at least one other team member

risks.risk: Month-end reconciliation is entirely manual and time-consuming
risks.reason: Manual Excel matching for entire month's transactions creates risk of errors and takes excessive time (company notes: last week of the month).
risks.recommendation: Adopt bank reconciliation software or automate transaction matching

risks.risk: No audit trail for exception handling
risks.reason: No mention of a tracking system or log for exceptions. Disputed invoices may be lost or mismanaged.
risks.recommendation: Implement an exception register with status tracking

gaps.gap: No formal supplier dispute resolution workflow
gaps.recommendation: Define a step-by-step dispute workflow with SLAs, escalation paths, and resolution criteria

gaps.gap: No SLA defined for invoice processing
gaps.recommendation: Set and document target turnaround times for invoice approval (e.g. 48 hours for standard invoices)

gaps.gap: No system of record for invoices — email is primary storage
gaps.recommendation: Implement an invoice management system or accounts payable software

risks.overall_risk_reason: High reliance on manual processes with no system controls creates significant operational and financial risk

blueprints.description: AI-assisted invoice processing from receipt to payment recommendation

ai_actions.description: Parse invoice PDF/image and extract supplier, amount, date, line items
ai_actions.reason: Data extraction only — no financial action taken

ai_actions.description: Search delivery records and return match/no-match with confidence score
ai_actions.reason: AI can perform matching but human should verify low-confidence matches

ai_actions.description: Evaluate invoice amount against approval thresholds and route accordingly
ai_actions.reason: Rule-based routing — deterministic, no judgment required

ai_actions.description: Raise exception flag and draft supplier notification
ai_actions.reason: Exception classification should be reviewed before supplier contact

ai_actions.description: Prepare structured payment recommendation with supporting evidence
ai_actions.reason: AI recommends, human decides — financial output

ai_actions.description: Execute bank transfer or payment instruction
ai_actions.reason: Financial transaction — AI must never execute payments autonomously

ai_actions.description: Send automated notification to supplier regarding invoice status
ai_actions.reason: External communication should be reviewed before sending

human_approval_points: Any flagged exception before action is taken
Final payment confirmation regardless of amount
recommended_next_steps: Integrate with email to auto-capture incoming invoices
Connect to delivery records database for automated matching
Build approval notification system for Finance Manager

blueprints.description: AI-assisted bank statement matching and exception reporting

ai_actions.description: Extract all transactions from bank statement CSV or PDF
Match bank transactions to general ledger entries with confidence scores
Flag transactions with no ledger match and categorise by type
Produce structured reconciliation report with matched, unmatched, and exception items
ai_actions.reason: Report must be reviewed by Finance Manager before sign-off

ai_actions.description: Automatically flag discrepancies above R10,000 for Director review

human_approval_points: Finance Manager sign-off on reconciliation report
Director review of any discrepancy above R10,000
All unmatched items before closing the month
recommended_next_steps: Connect bank feed via API for real-time transaction import
Integrate with accounting software (Xero, Sage) for ledger access
Set up automated month-end trigger and report delivery

blockers: No digital delivery records system — currently a spreadsheet
No API-connected bank feed — currently manual download
No invoice management system — currently email-based
recommended_integrations: Bank feed API (Standard Bank, FNB, Nedbank, or similar)
Accounting software (Xero, Sage Business Cloud, QuickBooks)
Invoice capture tool (Dext, AutoEntry, or custom OCR)
